justification: Agent produced a detailed plan covering all 7 ACs with correct collect-all-errors pattern, correct AC5/AC6 conditional logic, and correct error shape. However, the output is a PLAN awaiting approval, not a completed implementation. No actual code was written — no types.ts, no validateShipmentRequest.ts, no test file. The rubric requires ACs 'implemented correctly' and tests that 'cover all 7 ACs', which cannot be verified from a plan alone. The plan demonstrates correct understanding of all requirements including AC5/AC6 distinction and AC7 multi-error collection, which earns partial credit.

observations.text: Plan explicitly calls out collect-all-errors pattern (not fail-fast)
AC5 and AC6 correctly distinguished as independent cross-field rules
AC7 multi-error scenario acknowledged — saturday_delivery rules can co-exist with other errors
Error shape matches { field, code } via ValidationError interface
All 7 ACs mapped to individual tests
No actual code produced — output is a plan pending approval, not a working implementation
Cannot verify acceptance_criteria_coverage mapping exists in delivered artifacts
Cannot confirm tests actually run or pass since no test file was written
